2604.15009.title: Towards Faster Language Model Inference Using Mixture-of-Experts Flow Matching
2604.15009.paper_summary: Flow matching retains the generation quality of diffusion models while enabling substantially faster inference, making it a compelling paradigm for generative modeling. However, when applied to language modeling, it exhibits fundamental limitations in representing complex latent distributions with irregular geometries, such as anisotropy and multimodality. To address these challenges, we propose a mixture-of-experts flow matching (MoE-FM) framework, which captures complex global transport geometries in latent space by decomposing them into locally specialized vector fields. Building on MoE-FM, we develop a non-autoregressive (NAR) language modeling approach, named YAN, instantiated with both Transformer and Mamba architectures. Across multiple downstream tasks, YAN achieves generation quality on par with both autoregressive (AR) and diffusion-based NAR language models, while requiring as few as three sampling steps. This yields a $40\times$ speedup over AR baselines and up to a $10^3\times$ speedup over diffusion language models, demonstrating substantial efficiency advantages for language modeling.
2604.15009.paper_summary_zh: 流匹配保留了擴散模型的生成質量，同時實現了顯著更快的推理，使其成為生成建模的一種引人注目的範式。<lb/>然而，當應用於語言建模時，它在表示具有不規則幾何形狀的複雜潛在分佈方面顯示出根本性的限制，例如各向異性和多模態性。<lb/>為了解決這些挑戰，我們提出了一種專家混合流匹配（MoE-FM）框架，通過將複雜的全局傳輸幾何結構分解為局部專門的向量場來捕捉潛在空間中的複雜結構。<lb/>基於MoE-FM，我們開發了一種非自回歸（NAR）語言建模方法，名為YAN，並以Transformer和Mamba架構進行實現。<lb/>在多個下游任務中，YAN的生成質量與自回歸（AR）和基於擴散的NAR語言模型相當，同時僅需三次取樣步驟。<lb/>這使得相較於AR基準實現了$40\times$的加速，並且相較於擴散語言模型實現了高達$10^3\times$的加速，顯示出語言建模的顯著效率優勢。
2604.15009.author: Aihua Li et.al.
